subject: Statistics
title: Absolute vs. Relative Risk
description: A micro-lesson that makes Absolute vs. Relative Risk usable.

hook.text: 'Drug reduces heart attack risk by 50%!' Sounds incredible. But 50% of what?

buildup.text: If risk drops from 2% to 1%, the relative reduction is 50%. But the absolute reduction is just 1 percentage point.

discovery.text: Relative risk sounds dramatic. Absolute risk tells you what actually changed for an individual patient.

twist.text: Drug ads always use relative risk. It sounds bigger. '50% reduction' sells better than '1% reduction.'

climax.text: Always ask: 50% of what? Without the baseline, relative risk is meaningless marketing.

punchline.text: Relative risk sells. Absolute risk tells the truth.

quiz.question: Why is absolute risk more informative than relative risk?
options: It tells you the actual change in probability for an individual, not just the ratio
It doesn't require a control group
